_meta.dna: billing(pipeline) = signup(google) x verify(email) x subscribe(stripe) x credits(manage) x upgrade(tier) x cancel(clean)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor has 7 seed questions from a specific persona context. Questions must be answerable by checking if something actually works — click it, see it, verify it. Not theoretical.

name: Tower of Auth + Stripe + Billing
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT(7q) -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

floors.persona_context: 30, Berlin, Frontend developer, Expert tech, Free tier, signing up for Solace for the first time using Google account
questions: Can I click 'Sign in with Google' on the Solace login page and see the Google OAuth consent screen within 2 seconds?
After approving the Google sign-in, does the app redirect me to my Solace dashboard with my Google name and profile photo displayed?
If I already have a Solace account with a different email, does the sign-in flow detect the conflict and offer to link accounts?
Does the sign-in work if my Google account has 2FA enabled (TOTP or security key)?
If I click 'Sign in with Google' and then cancel the OAuth consent, does the app return me to the login page with no error state?
Is the Google sign-in button styled correctly (following Google's branding guidelines) and not a generic 'Login' button?
After sign-in, can I verify in my browser's network tab that no Google credentials were sent to any third-party servers?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Nina is security-conscious and technically savvy. The Google sign-in must follow OAuth best practices, handle edge cases, and be visually correct.

floors.persona_context: 43, Sao Paulo, Backend engineer evaluating Solace for his team, Expert tech, evaluating Enterprise tier, needs to understand the auth architecture
questions: After Google sign-in, does the backend exchange the Google ID token for a Firebase custom token within 1 second?
Can I see in the network tab that the Firebase token is a JWT with correct claims (uid, email, tier)?
Does the Firebase token expire after a reasonable TTL (1 hour) and auto-refresh without logging me out?
If the Firebase token exchange fails (backend down), does the app show a specific error rather than a blank screen?
Is the Firebase token stored in an httpOnly secure cookie or secure local storage — not in a plain cookie or URL parameter?
Does the /auth/verify endpoint return 401 with a clear error message when I send an expired or invalid token?
Can I log out and verify that the Firebase token is deleted from storage and the /auth/verify endpoint rejects it?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Roberto is evaluating auth security. Every question must be verifiable by inspecting network requests, storage, and error responses. Architecture diagrams alone are not sufficient.

floors.persona_context: 35, Chicago, Marketing agency owner, Intermediate tech, upgrading from Free to Pro tier, has a company credit card
questions: Can I click 'Upgrade to Pro' on the pricing page and be redirected to a Stripe Checkout session with the correct $28/month price?
Does the Stripe Checkout page show my email pre-filled and the plan name 'Solace Pro' with monthly billing?
After entering my credit card and clicking Pay, does the app redirect me to /billing/success with a confirmation and receipt?
If I close the Stripe Checkout tab without paying, does the app show my account still on Free tier — no orphaned subscription?
Does the Stripe Checkout support Apple Pay and Google Pay as alternative payment methods?
After successful payment, does my account tier update to Pro immediately (not after a manual admin action)?
Can I find a receipt for my payment in my email inbox within 2 minutes of completing Stripe Checkout?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Ashley is paying with a company card. Stripe Checkout must be seamless, show correct pricing, handle abandonment, and activate the tier instantly.

floors.persona_context: 51, Osaka, IT consultant, Expert tech, Pro tier, monitors LLM token usage carefully, bought $5 credits top-up
questions: Can I see my current credit balance displayed in the dashboard header showing both dollar amount and estimated tasks remaining?
Does the credits panel show a breakdown of credit usage by app (Gmail Triage: $0.42, LinkedIn Poster: $0.18) for the current billing period?
Can I click 'Buy Credits' and purchase a $5 top-up through Stripe without changing my subscription tier?
After buying credits, does the balance update immediately in the dashboard without requiring a page refresh?
Does the credits panel show a usage graph over the last 30 days so I can see if my spending is increasing?
If my credit balance drops below $1, does the app show a warning banner with a link to buy more credits?
Can I set a monthly spending cap so the app stops executing recipes when I hit my budget limit?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Hiroshi tracks every yen. The credits panel must be transparent, real-time, and provide spending controls. Surprise charges or unclear usage breakdowns are deal-breakers.

floors.persona_context: 28, Paris, Startup co-founder, Intermediate tech, currently on Starter $8/month, needs cloud twin features available in Pro $28/month
questions: Can I see a comparison table of Starter vs Pro features on the pricing page showing exactly what I gain by upgrading?
When I click 'Upgrade to Pro', does Stripe prorate my current billing cycle so I am not double-charged for this month?
After upgrading, can I immediately access Pro features (cloud twin, OAuth3 vault) without logging out and back in?
Does my billing page show the upgrade date, next billing date, and the new monthly amount?
If I upgrade mid-cycle, does the billing page explain the prorated amount clearly (not just a weird charge amount)?
Can I downgrade from Pro back to Starter and see which features I will lose before confirming?
Does downgrading take effect at the end of the current billing cycle (not immediately) so I do not lose access mid-month?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Chloe is a founder watching cash flow. Upgrade/downgrade must be transparent about prorating, feature changes, and timing. Surprise charges destroy trust.

floors.persona_context: 60, Denver, Retired accountant, Beginner tech, Pro tier, cancelling because he wants to try the Free tier first
questions: Can I find the 'Cancel Plan' option in my account settings without needing to contact support or send an email?
Does the cancellation flow show me what I will lose (cloud twin, vault sync) and what I keep (local data, evidence chains)?
After clicking Cancel, does the app confirm that my Pro access continues until the end of the current billing period?
Does the cancellation send a confirmation email to my registered email address with the cancellation date?
After cancellation takes effect, does my account gracefully downgrade to Free — not lock me out of the app entirely?
Can I resubscribe to Pro after cancelling without losing my previously saved evidence chains and app configurations?
If I cancel within 24 hours of subscribing, does the app offer a full refund or at least explain the refund policy?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Marcus is non-technical and cancelling. The cancellation flow must be easy to find, transparent about what happens, and not punitive. Dark patterns (hiding cancel, guilt-tripping) are BANNED.

floors.persona_context: 33, Krakow, DevOps engineer, Expert tech, Pro tier with BYOK, manages her own Anthropic and OpenAI API keys
questions: Can I navigate to Settings > API Keys and add my Anthropic API key, seeing it stored with only the last 4 characters visible?
After adding my BYOK key, does the app route all LLM requests through my key instead of the managed LLM service?
Can I add both an Anthropic key and an OpenAI key and choose which one to use per app or per recipe?
Does the app validate my API key on save by making a test request and showing 'Key valid' or 'Key invalid' status?
If my API key is revoked or expires, does the app show a clear error on the next recipe run rather than silently falling back to managed LLM?
Can I delete my API key from settings and verify it is removed from local encrypted storage (not just hidden in the UI)?
Does using BYOK disable the managed LLM billing line item on my invoice so I am only paying $8 base?
floors.question_expansion_instruction: Lena manages her own API keys. BYOK must be truly bring-your-own with no hidden charges, proper key validation, and fail-loud behavior when keys expire.
